name: Wu An
page_title: Wu An 吳安 [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: Executed for being associated with Sun Ba.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	吳安


	 Pronunciation 
	Wu2 An1


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Wú Ān


	 Wade-Giles 
	Wu An
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Wu An was from Wu county, Wu commandery. He was the son of Wu Fen.

After the heir to the Wu empire, Sun Deng, died in 241, Sun Quan made Sun He the heir. However, Sun Ba also had a good claim to being the heir, and factions formed in favor of either two. Wu An belonged to the Sun Ba faction. He constantly pandered to the prince and slandered his enemies, including Sun He. Sun Quan was becoming increasingly irritated with the factional turmoil and in 250, he forced Sun Ba to commit suicide and deposed Sun He. In the aftermath, Wu An was executed.

full_text:
Wu An 吳安
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Wu An (吳安). Executed for being associated with Sun Ba. Wu An was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Wu An lived during the Three Kingdoms period of Chinese history.

Wu An was from Wu county, Wu commandery. He was the son of Wu Fen.
After the heir to the Wu empire, Sun Deng, died in 241, Sun Quan made Sun He the heir. However, Sun Ba also had a good claim to being the heir, and factions formed in favor of either two. Wu An belonged to the Sun Ba faction. He constantly pandered to the prince and slandered his enemies, including Sun He. Sun Quan was becoming increasingly irritated with the factional turmoil and in 250, he forced Sun Ba to commit suicide and deposed Sun He. In the aftermath, Wu An was executed.
